Sprint 12: Mehr Interceptoren (subprocess, ftplib, paramiko, Rate-Limiting)

- schemas.py: +Verb.EXECUTE, +Tool.SHELL/FTP/SSH
- risk_engine.py: Verb.EXECUTE Impact = 95 (höchstes Risiko)
- policy_engine.py: +PRR-009 (EXECUTE → immer DENY, Living-off-the-land Schutz)
- config.py: +rate_limit_max_actions (20), +rate_limit_window_seconds (60)
- gate.py: Rate-Limiting mit deque rolling window, RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED DENY
- interceptor.py: subprocess.run/Popen/call/check_*/ftplib/paramiko abgefangen
- wrapper.py: +ig.shell, ig.ftp, ig.ssh Sub-Wrappers + async Varianten
- owasp.py: +PRR-009 → LLM06 Mapping

commit.message:
fix: Terrain rendering — full sphere coverage, no grid artifacts (#752)

- Two-phase LOD: min depth 2 for full coverage, then priority subdivision
- Patch overlap (1%) instead of skirts to fill seam gaps without grid lines
- Sphere normals for seamless lighting across patch boundaries
- Remove body quaternion from terrain group (procedural terrain, no alignment needed)
- Conservative back-face culling (dot < -0.6)
- FrontSide rendering, 800 patch budget, 1024 pool
- Fixed shader: world-space normals/lighting, pre-scaled maria threshold

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: output format per-config persistence (#23), font options + weight + antialiasing (#26)

Issue #23: Output settings now sync to backend on every change
- Import syncPipelineToBackend in OutputSection.svelte
- Add syncPipelineToBackend() calls to all output setting changes:
  - save_to_file checkbox onchange
  - save_to_database checkbox onchange
  - database_path input onblur
  - include_response checkbox onchange
  - output_directory input onblur
  - output_format input oninput
  - output_format_type SkeuSelect onValueChange
  - capture_filters add/remove/edit operations
- Fixes output format resetting to default on app restart

Issue #26: Enhanced font customization and rendering
- Add 5 new font options: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, Source Code Pro, Comic Code, Courier New
- Add Font Weight selector (Light 300, Regular 400, Medium 500)
- Add fontWeight to app state (default '400')
- Add font_weight field to GuiConfig Rust struct
- Wire font_weight save/load through IPC handlers
- Apply --font-weight CSS variable in +page.svelte
- Add CSS font antialiasing properties: -webkit-font-smoothing, -moz-osx-font-smoothing, text-rendering
- Update --font-mono CSS var to include new fonts
- Fixes text appearing too bold/smeared at 140% scale + 16px

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

relay: fix chicken-and-egg dep install via rebuild.sh

package.json "build" now calls scripts/rebuild.sh which handles
npm install before tsc. Since the old relay runs `npm run build`
and package.json is pulled via git, this works retroactively —
no need to SSH into ships to install new dependencies.

Also: gitSync detects package-lock.json changes and pre-installs
deps before returning, as a belt-and-suspenders measure.

commit.message:
fix(parity): uhitm.js ad_dren/ad_slee RNG gates + muse.js djinni rn2(2)

- mhitm_ad_dren: add missing !rn2(4) gate (C: 25% chance to drain energy)
  and attacktype(AT_MAGC/AT_BREA) check matching C's xdrainenergym
- mhitm_ad_slee: consume rnd(10) before resists_sleep check (C argument
  eval order), add second rnd(10) when sleep succeeds (C's double
  sleep_monst call in && chain)
- muse.js precheck: add rn2(2) after smoky djinni makemon to decide
  peaceful vs vanish (was completely missing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Show desktop toolbar rows in mobile landscape orientation

Many phones are still ≤768px wide in landscape (iPhone SE=667px,
iPhone 12 mini=740px), so the mobile breakpoint was hiding the
toolbar rows even in landscape. Added orientation:landscape nested
media query to show toolbar rows (compact, no labels) and hide the
mobile bottom bar and floating zoom controls in landscape.

feat: implement Phase 2 — complete service mesh

Alert.API
- Full alert lifecycle: Open → Acknowledged → Resolved
- RabbitMQ consumer for MetricThresholdBreachedEvent
- Publishes AlertTriggeredEvent downstream
- REST endpoints: list, get, summary, acknowledge, resolve
- EF Core + SmartFactory_Alerts DB, Prometheus metrics

Notification.API
- SignalR hub at /hubs/factory for real-time push to Angular
- Consumers for AlertTriggeredEvent and EquipmentStatusChangedEvent
- Notification log (NotificationLog) with read/unread tracking
- REST endpoints: recent, summary, mark-read, mark-all-read
- EF Core + SmartFactory_Notifications DB

Identity.API
- Self-contained JWT auth (PBKDF2 password hashing, no external IdP)
- RBAC: Admin, Engineer, Operator, Viewer roles
- Seeded users: admin, engineer, operator
- POST /api/auth/login → JWT token
- UsersController (admin-only CRUD + deactivate)

Infrastructure
- docker-compose: added alert-api (5003), notification-api (5004), identity-api (5005)
- prometheus.yml: scrapes all three new services
- API gateway: added routes for /api/alerts, /api/notifications, /hubs (SignalR), /api/auth, /api/users
- .gitignore: exclude memory/ directory

feat: add PR review agent with independent model and auto-merge support

Add a dedicated pr-reviewer agent that independently reviews PRs created
by the coder agent, using its own configurable model/provider. The review
agent submits GitHub PR reviews (approve/request-changes) and can
optionally auto-merge approved PRs when CI checks pass.

Key changes:
- New pr-reviewer agent with focused review prompt (no handoffs)
- Config: prReview.provider, model, autoMerge, maxReviewCycles, requireChecks
- GitHub client: submitPRReview, mergePR, allChecksPassed, fetchPRReviews
- Orchestrator: full review lifecycle with cycle tracking and human escalation
- Labels: oneagent-needs-review (trigger), oneagent-needs-human (escalation)
- Manual trigger: add oneagent-needs-review label to any PR

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle SMSMasivos verification_04 (already verified number)

instead of OTP input on the consent screen.

Harden web_fetch and add search cache (#20)

## Summary
- **web_fetch hardening**: HTML→markdown (links, headings, lists),
structured JSON output, redirect limit (5), response size guard (2MB),
browser User-Agent, http/https-only, JSON pretty-print

between Brave and DuckDuckGo
- **15 new tests** (299 total): cache TTL/eviction, htmlToMarkdown,
structured output, protocol validation, size limits, redirect following

- [x] `npm test` — 299 tests pass

fix(tls): replace silent cert error swallow with specific catch (#6)

* fix(tls): replace silent cert error swallow with specific catch and warning

- Replace `with _ -> false` on `Ssl.set_default_verify_paths` with proper
  error handling: log warning on exception (with exception details), and
  also warn when the function returns false (CA certs failed to load).
- Unexpected exceptions now propagate with their message logged to stderr
  instead of being silently swallowed.
- When Verify_peer is set but CA certs fail to load, operators now see a
  clear warning rather than silent verification bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tls): fail-closed when system CA certificates unavailable

Previously, create_ssl_context returned Some ctx even when
Ssl.set_default_verify_paths failed, effectively disabling peer
verification (fail-open). Now returns None, causing connect to
reject the TLS connection with a clear error message.

commit.message:
feat: add 5 new tools bringing total to 75 — auto_place, pcb_pipeline, set_board_design_rules, get_footprint_bounds, get_pcb_workflow

New tools
─────────
• get_footprint_bounds (library.py) — returns courtyard {xmin,ymin,xmax,ymax},
  width_mm, height_mm, and pads list for any footprint before placing it.
  Uses _parse_footprint_bounds (file_backend.py) which walks balanced parens
  to correctly extract nested S-expression blocks (regex .*?\) fails here).

• set_board_design_rules (board.py / FileBoardOps) — writes IPC-2221 Class 2
  or JLCPCB 2-layer standard rules into the board's (setup ...) section so DRC
  enforces real manufacturing constraints instead of KiCad permissive defaults.
  Presets: "class2" (0.20 mm clearance, 0.25 mm trace, 0.30 mm via drill),
           "fab_jlcpcb" (0.10 mm clearance, 0.127 mm trace, 0.20 mm via drill).

• auto_place (board.py) — geometry-driven bin-packing placement. Reads courtyard
  extents for every footprint, sorts components by class
  (connectors→ICs→discretes→transistors→LEDs→others), packs rows with a
  guaranteed courtyard-to-courtyard gap ≥ clearance_mm. Falls back to 5×5 mm
  stub when a footprint is not found in system libraries (with warning).

• pcb_pipeline (board.py) — full schematic-to-routed-PCB in one call:
  sync_schematic_to_pcb → set_board_design_rules → add Edge.Cuts gr_rect →
  auto_place → autoroute (FreeRouting) → run_drc. Calls _impl_* helpers from
  routing.py directly to avoid FunctionTool-is-not-callable trap.

• get_pcb_workflow (project.py) — returns a structured 11-step JSON workflow
  reference so AI assistants follow the correct tool sequence from project
  creation through Gerber export.

Other changes
─────────────
• read_schematic / read_board: new optional `include` parameter to filter which
  sections are returned (reduces token usage for large designs).
• get_symbol_pin_positions: new `references` list parameter for batch queries.
• sync_schematic_to_pcb / compare_schematic_pcb: multi-unit component merge
  (preserves footprint from whichever unit carries it).
• run_drc / run_erc / compare_schematic_pcb / get_net_connections /
  sync_schematic_to_pcb: apply limit_response() to cap large violation lists.
• search_library_sources: apply limit_response() to cap symbol/footprint lists.
• File format version bumps: schematics 20250114, symbol libs 20241209,
  PCB files 20241229 (matches KiCad 9 output).
• KiCad 9 s-expression fixes: (hide yes) instead of bare hide keyword;
  (exclude_from_sim no) field added to add_component and add_power_symbol.
• Tests: assert 75 tools; update version string assertion to 20250114.
• Fixtures: bump version strings to match new format output.
